data.title: Roper Techs Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
data.description:
Roper Techs (NASDAQ:ROP) reported first-quarter financial results on Thursday. The transcript from the company's first-quarter earnings call has been provided below.

Benzinga APIs provide real-time access to earnings call transcripts and financial data. Visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/ to learn more.

The full earnings call is available at https://edge.media-server.com/mmc/p/fnvwxvsv/

Summary

Roper Techs reported strong financial performance in Q1 2026, with total revenue growth of 11%, organic revenue growth of 6%, and EBITDA growth of 8%. The company raised its full-year DEPS guidance by $0.50 at the midpoint.

The company is accelerating AI integration across its portfolio, with significant advancements in businesses like Central Reach, ConstructConnect, and Vertafore. The AI Accelerator team is making notable progress, particularly with Vertafore's new AI agents.

Roper Techs completed substantial share repurchases, with 6 million shares bought back since November, and the board authorized an additional $3 billion for future repurchases, providing significant capital deployment flexibility.

Full Transcript

OPERATOR

Good morning. The Roper Technologies conference call will now begin. Today's call is being recorded. All participants will be in listen only mode. Should you need assistance, please signal a conference specialist by pressing zero. I would now like to turn the call over to Zach Moxie, Vice President, Investor Relations. Please go ahead.

Zach Moxie (Vice President, Investor Relations)

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

OPERATOR

Dylan Becker (Analyst)

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Dylan Becker (Analyst)

Great, thank you very much.

Rhea

Your next question comes from Brent Thiel with Jeffries. Your line is now open. Hi, this is Rhea on for Brentville. Thank you for taking the question. Neil. Just curious to hear your thoughts on the private markets given ongoing volatility. Can you just tell us a little bit more about what you're seeing right now and if it's changed your outlook at all?

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Yeah, and I would just reiterate, we refinanced our five year revolver this quarter at a very good cost of capital. Sort of tightened up the spread a little bit. So shout out To Shannon and Dave Baker for getting that done this quarter. Just a great job there and you know, just positioned us well. We have a lot of balance sheet flexibility and we'll be able to move quickly when the opportunities arise.

Rhea

Joseph Vruink (Analyst)

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Joseph Vruink (Analyst)

Great, that's helpful. And then I heard enterprise bookings upload double digits over the trailing 12 months. Curious what they were in the quarter. And I think your definition excludes price. Maybe. Can you just comment on pricing power in the aggregate?

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Terry Tillman (Analyst)

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Terry Tillman (Analyst)

Thanks a lot.

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Yeah. Final thing I'd say on that is I think it's kind of from an investment point of view. It's probably more of a reallocation or rebalancing of investment from customer support, customer service to fte. So I don't know if it's like a huge cost increase. It's just a resource allocation dynamic.

Joe Giordano (Analyst)

Got it. Thanks. Your next question comes from Joe Giordano with TD Cowan. Your line is now open.

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Hey guys. Morning. Morning. Just curious on your talk about like embeddability and subscription plus overage in the future.

Joe Giordano (Analyst)

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Joe Giordano (Analyst)

Yeah, that's kind of what I'm getting at. More of the ROI from Roper's standpoint, I get how I get the ROI from the customers. It's more like if we're spending money, develop new AI tools that are then embedded in the product that we're already offering. Like, how's the ROI on the increased investment you need in 2026 versus the investment you needed, you know, in 2021, to get the same customer and keep the same customer?

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

George Kuro Sawa (Analyst)

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

George Kuro Sawa (Analyst)

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

That's right, yeah. And I think the benefit of being part of Roper, we set our president summit a couple months ago and we did an AI sort of showcase for those further along. It just helps with the learning acceleration. But I would agree with Neil that it's those that embraced and saw a true customer problem early on and then got after it a little sooner. But others are coming up the curve very quickly.

Clark Jeffries (Analyst)

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Josh Tilton

Your next question comes from Josh Tilton with Wolf Research. Your line is now open.

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Hey guys, thanks for sneaking me in here and congrats on a really strong start to the year. I will keep it to one given

Ken Wong

the hour, but my question is just basically you're very clear that the guidance still doesn't assume a recovery at Delltec and DAT for the rest of the year. Can you just remind us the confidence that you have in the rest of the application and network software business and

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Julian Mitchell (Analyst)

Thank you guys. Your next question comes from Ken Wong with Oppenheimer. Your line is now open. Great. Like Josh, thanks for sneaking me in. Just one. For me it sounds like kind of the downtick in 2Q is just purely due to tough comps, but just wanted to kind of make sure and clarify any geopolitical macro dynamics that you guys baked into that assumption as well given kind of the current situation that arose.

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Julian Mitchell (Analyst)

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

Julian Mitchell (Analyst)

Kenny Simon

That's great. Thank you. Your next question comes from Dean Dray with RBC Capital Market. Your line is now open. Thank you. This is Kenny Simon for Dean. I wanted to ask about Neptune business. So one of your peers has some meaningful project delays disruption in the quarter for their water meter business. Have you seen anything similar in terms of the industry dynamic or even any market share changes during the quarter?

Neil Hun (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Jason Conley (Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer)

No, I think that you covered it. Thanks.

OPERATOR

Thank you.

Zach Moxie (Vice President, Investor Relations)

This concludes our question and answer session. We will now return back to Zach Moxie for any closing remarks.

OPERATOR

Thanks, everyone, for joining us today. We look forward to speaking with you during our next earnings call.

data.title: How To Earn $500 A Month From HCA Healthcare Stock Ahead Of Q1 Earnings
data.description:
HCA Healthcare, Inc. (NYSE:HCA) will release earnings for its first quarter before the opening bell on Friday, April 24.

Analysts expect the company to report quarterly earnings of $7.15 per share. That’s up from $6.45 per share in the year-ago period. The consensus estimate for HCA Healthcare's quarterly revenue is $19.07 billion (it reported $18.32 billion last year), according to Benzinga Pro.

With the recent buzz around HCA Healthcare, some investors may be eyeing potential gains from the company's dividends too. Currently, HCA has an annual dividend yield of 0.66%, which is a quarterly dividend amount of 78 cents per share ($3.12 per year).  

So, how can investors exploit its dividend yield to pocket a regular $500 monthly?

To earn $500 per month or $6,000 annually from dividends alone, you would need an investment of approximately $906,387 or around 1,923 shares. For a more modest $100 per month or $1,200 per year, you would need $181,466 or around 385 shares.

To calculate: Divide the desired annual income ($6,000 or $1,200) by the dividend ($3.12 in this case). So, $6,000 / $3.12 = 1,923 ($500 per month), and $1,200 / $3.12 = 385 shares ($100 per month).

Note that dividend yield can change on a rolling basis, as the dividend payment and the stock price both fluctuate over time.

How that works: The dividend yield is computed by dividing the annual dividend payment by the stock’s current price.

For example, if a stock pays an annual dividend of $2 and is currently priced at $50, the dividend yield would be 4% ($2/$50). However, if the stock price increases to $60, the dividend yield drops to 3.33% ($2/$60). Conversely, if the stock price falls to $40, the dividend yield rises to 5% ($2/$40).

Similarly, changes in the dividend payment can impact the yield. If a company increases its dividend, the yield will also increase, provided the stock price stays the same. Conversely, if the dividend payment decreases, so will the yield.

HCA Price Action: Shares of HCA Healthcare rose slightly to close at $471.34 on Wednesday.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Transcript: American Express Q1 2026 Earnings Conference Call
data.description:
American Express (NYSE:AXP) released first-quarter financial results and hosted an earnings call on Thursday. Read the complete transcript below.

This transcript is brought to you by Benzinga APIs. For real-time access to our entire catalog, please visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/ for a consultation.

The full earnings call is available at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/197664405

Summary

American Express reported a strong Q1 2026 with revenue growth of 11% and an EPS increase of 18% year-over-year.

The company plans to increase investments in marketing and technology due to strong performance and confidence in future growth.

American Express reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance of 9 to 10% revenue growth and EPS between $17.30 and $17.90.

Notable initiatives include a multi-year global partnership with the NFL and the launch of new commercial products for businesses.

Management highlighted the ongoing success of the US Platinum card refresh and strong credit performance.

for the full year for us. Yeah, and I think if you look at the what we just announced, you know, you look at those, they're there are more, not a lot of additional benefits on those cards. data.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript
data.description:
Thermo Fisher Scientific (NYSE:TMO) reported first-quarter financial results on Thursday. The transcript from the company's first-quarter earnings call has been provided below.

This content is powered by Benzinga APIs. For comprehensive financial data and transcripts, visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/.

View the webcast at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/571753804

Summary

Thermo Fisher Scientific reported a 6% revenue growth in Q1 2026, reaching $11.01 billion, with adjusted EPS also up by 6% to $5.44.

The company completed the acquisition of Clario, enhancing its clinical research capabilities and contributing $30 million to Q1 revenue.

Thermo Fisher Scientific raised its 2026 full-year guidance, now expecting $47.3 billion to $48.1 billion in revenue and adjusted EPS between $24.64 and $25.12.

The pharma and biotech sectors showed strong growth, while academic and government revenues declined slightly due to macroeconomic conditions.

Strategic collaborations, such as with Nvidia, and product innovations in analytical instruments were highlighted as key growth drivers.

Management expressed confidence in navigating current macroeconomic challenges, including inflation, leveraging their PPI business system.

Full Transcript

To ask a question on today's call, please press STAR followed by one on your telephone keypad. If you change your mind, please press Star followed by two. data.title: Pool Corp Q1 Earnings: EPS And Sales Beat Estimates, Stock Gains On Raised Guidance
data.description:
• Pool shares are trending higher. Why is POOL stock trading higher?

POOL Q1 Earnings, Revenue Highlights

The wholesale distributor of swimming pool supplies reported first-quarter diluted earnings of $1.45 per share, representing a 2% year-over-year increase and beating the analyst consensus estimate of $1.36. Pool also posted quarterly net sales of $1.138 billion, which topped the consensus estimate of $1.097 billion.

Net sales climbed 6% year-over-year, driven by solid demand for maintenance products, strong equipment sales, and a gradual improvement in discretionary categories. Year-over-year sales growth also benefited from price increases enacted last year.

While operating income grew 7% to $82.6 million, gross margin decreased 20 basis points to 29.0% due to a typical seasonal mix of lower-margin customer early buy purchases.

POOL Stock 2026 Guidance And Outlook

Looking ahead, Pool Corporation updated its full-year 2026 GAAP earnings guidance from a previous range of $10.85 to $11.15 to a raised range of $10.87 to $11.17 per share, sitting near the current analyst estimate of $11.11.

CEO Peter D. Arvan highlighted the company’s strong start, noting resilient maintenance demand and confidence in driving profitable growth.

POOL Shares Edge Higher Thursday Morning

POOL Price Action: Pool shares were up 3.65% at $242.78 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock
